2606.24200.title: MMed-Bench-IR: A Heterogeneous Benchmark for Multilingual Medical Information Retrieval
2606.24200.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in clinical settings increasingly requires multilingual retrieval against predominantly English evidence corpora. Multilingual medical retrieval demands three capabilities: cross-lingual alignment, concept discrimination, and evidence retrieval. However, existing benchmarks evaluate these only in isolation, leaving the interaction between biomedical expertise and multilingual coverage unmeasured. We introduce MMed-Bench-IR, a benchmark designed to disentangle these axes across 6 languages and three structurally heterogeneous tasks: (1) cross-lingual medical QA retrieval with 6,127 queries grounded in the Unified Medical Language System (UMLS), (2) concept discrimination over 4,975 confusion sets at three difficulty tiers, and (3) multilingual evidence retrieval for RAG with 2,040 quality-assured queries. The three tasks share zero concept and query overlap by design, ensuring that aggregate scores reflect genuine capability breadth. Evaluation of ten systems across six paradigm families reveals severe cross-lingual failure: biomedical encoders that score 0.818 nDCG@10 in English drop to 0.056 in Japanese, a gap that English-only benchmarks cannot detect.
2606.24200.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）在臨床環境中越來越需要針對主要為英語的證據語料庫進行多語言檢索。多語言醫療檢索需要三項能力：跨語言對齊、概念區分和證據檢索。然而，現有的基準僅孤立地評估這些能力，未能測量生物醫學專業知識與多語言覆蓋之間的互動。我們介紹了MMed-Bench-IR，這是一個旨在拆解這些軸的基準，涵蓋6種語言和三個結構異質的任務：（1）基於統一醫學語言系統（UMLS）的6,127個查詢的跨語言醫療問答檢索，（2）在三個難度層級上對4,975個混淆集進行概念區分，以及（3）針對RAG的多語言證據檢索，包含2,040個經質量保證的查詢。這三個任務在設計上沒有任何概念和查詢的重疊，確保總體得分反映真實的能力廣度。在六個範式家族中評估十個系統顯示出嚴重的跨語言失敗：在英語中得分為0.818 nDCG@10的生物醫學編碼器在日語中降至0.056，這一差距是僅基於英語的基準無法檢測到的。
2606.24200.author: Junhyeok Lee et.al.
2606.24200.authors: Junhyeok Lee, Han Jang, Hyeonjin Goh, Kyu Sung Choi
